definition_cn: 损失厌恶 - 人们对损失的痛苦感大于对等量收益的愉悦感
definition_en: People feel the pain of losses more acutely than the pleasure of equivalent gains; losses loom larger than gains
example_en: Investors refuse to sell losing stocks to avoid realizing losses, even when selling would be rational
example_cn: 投资者拒绝卖出亏损股票以避免确认损失，即使卖出是理性的

definition_cn: 锚定效应 - 过度依赖最先获得的信息（锚）做决策
definition_en: The tendency to rely too heavily on the first piece of information encountered when making decisions
example_en: Restaurant menu prices: a $50 dish makes the $25 main course seem reasonably priced
example_cn: 餐厅菜单定价：一道50美元的菜使25美元的主菜显得价格合理

definition_cn: 现状偏见 - 偏好当前状态，回避变化的心理倾向
definition_en: The preference for current state of affairs; the tendency to do nothing or maintain current choices
example_en: Employees never changing their 401(k) investment allocations despite market changes
example_cn: 员工从不改变他们的401(k)投资配置，尽管市场发生变化

definition_cn: 心理账户 - 人们以非理性方式将钱分配到不同心理账户
definition_en: The tendency to categorize money differently based on its origin or intended use, rather than treating all money as fungible
example_en: Spending a tax refund freely while being frugal with regular income
example_cn: 随意花掉退税而对固定收入精打细算

definition_cn: 助推理论 - 通过设计选择架构引导人们做出更好决策
definition_en: A concept by Thaler and Sunstein that designs choice architecture to influence behavior while preserving freedom of choice
example_en: Placing healthy foods at eye level in cafeterias increases their selection
example_cn: 将健康食品放在自助餐厅的视线高度会增加它们被选择的概率

definition_cn: 默认选项效应 - 人们倾向于接受预设的默认选项
definition_en: The tendency to accept pre-set options rather than make active choices; 'going with the flow'
example_en: Most employees stay in default 401(k) contribution rates rather than actively choosing their own
example_cn: 大多数员工保持默认的401(k)缴款比例，而不是主动选择自己的比例

definition_cn: 现时偏见 - 过度看重即时奖励而低估未来收益
definition_en: The tendency to overvalue immediate rewards relative to future rewards; difficulty in delaying gratification
example_en: Choosing a smaller payment now over a larger payment next month
example_cn: 选择现在获得较少付款而不是下个月获得较多付款

definition_cn: 过度自信偏差 - 高估自己的知识、能力和判断准确性
definition_en: The tendency to overestimate one's own abilities, knowledge, or chances of success
example_en: 90% of drivers consider themselves above-average drivers, which is statistically impossible
example_cn: 90%的司机认为自己驾驶水平高于平均水平，这在统计上是不可能的

definition_cn: 沉没成本谬误 - 因为已投入资源而继续不合理的决策
definition_en: Continuing a behavior because of previously invested resources (time, money, effort) rather than future value
example_en: Finishing a boring movie just because you already paid for the ticket
example_cn: 仅仅因为已经买了票而看完一部无聊的电影

definition_cn: 框架效应 - 决策受信息表述方式的影响
definition_en: How information is presented (positive vs. negative framing) significantly affects people's choices
example_en: '90% fat-free' sounds healthier than 'contains 10% fat' for the same product
example_cn: 听起来比
对同一产品更健康

definition_cn: 可得性启发法 - 根据容易想到的例子判断事件可能性
definition_en: Judging the likelihood of events based on how easily examples come to mind
example_en: Overestimating shark attack risk after seeing news coverage, despite being very rare
example_cn: 在看到新闻报道后高估鲨鱼袭击风险，尽管实际上非常罕见

definition_cn: 禀赋效应 - 对自己拥有的物品估值高于未拥有时
definition_en: Valuing items more highly when you own them than when you don't; reluctance to give up what we have
example_en: Refusing to sell your concert ticket for twice its face value because you 'already have it'
example_cn: 拒绝以两倍面值出售你的演唱会票，因为你

definition_cn: 从众行为 - 模仿他人决策而不独立分析
definition_en: Following the actions and decisions of a large group rather than making independent choices
example_en: Investors buying stocks during a bull market simply because 'everyone else is doing it'
example_cn: 投资者在牛市时仅仅因为
而购买股票

definition_cn: 选择过载 - 选项过多反而导致无法决策或满意度下降
definition_en: Having too many options leads to decision paralysis or decreased satisfaction with chosen option
example_en: Jam study: fewer flavors led to higher sales than 24 varieties
example_cn: 果酱研究：6种口味比24种口味带来更高销售

definition_cn: 乐观偏差 - 高估好事发生在自己身上的概率
definition_en: The tendency to believe that positive events are more likely to happen to us than to others
example_en: Most entrepreneurs believe their startup will succeed despite high failure rates
example_cn: 大多数企业家相信他们的创业会成功，尽管失败率很高

definition_cn: 双曲贴现 - 短期奖励的权重远高于长期奖励
definition_en: The tendency to prefer smaller immediate rewards over larger later rewards, inconsistent over time
example_en: Choosing $50 now over $100 in one year, but also choosing $100 in 2 years over $110 in 3 years
example_cn: 现在选择50美元而不是一年后获得100美元，但又选择两年后获得100美元而不是三年后获得110美元

definition_cn: 确认偏差 - 寻找证实已有信念的信息
definition_en: The tendency to search for and favor information that confirms pre-existing beliefs
example_en: Only reading news sources that support your political views
example_cn: 只阅读支持你政治观点的新闻来源

definition_cn: 赌徒谬误 - 认为随机序列会自我修正
definition_en: Believing that if something happens more frequently than normal, it will happen less frequently in the future (or vice versa)
example_en: After flipping 5 heads in a row, thinking tails is 'due' on the next flip
example_cn: 连续5次正面后，认为下一次翻转
是反面

definition_cn: 承诺装置 - 帮助克服短期诱惑的机制
definition_en: A tool or mechanism that helps individuals stick to their long-term goals by restricting future choices
example_en: Going to a café without Wi-Fi to finish writing, or paying a friend to hold you accountable
example_cn: 去没有Wi-Fi的咖啡馆写作，或付钱给朋友让你保持责任

definition_cn: 自由意志家长制 - 政府助推但不强制公众做出选择
definition_en: Thaler and Sunstein's concept: using nudges to help people while preserving their freedom to choose
example_en: Opt-out organ donation systems increase donation rates without mandating donation
example_cn: 选择退出器官捐献系统在不强制捐献的情况下提高捐献率
